summary: The portal-mobile overlay is structurally sound and ships in a usable, shop-ready state for mobile. The remaining opportunities are polish-oriented: make the input row visually balanced, reduce avatar dominance, improve the perceived usefulness of the transcript on first load, and tighten the chip row readability for narrow devices. These adjustments should elevate the perceived quality to a premium B2B feel without requiring a full structural rewrite. If these polish steps are completed, escalation to a full redo would be warranted only if the Mac-read remains negative after a second polish pass.

blockers.description: Input row controls are visually unbalanced: the textarea dominates the width and height, while the voice button and especially the Send button are comparatively small, making the composer look unfinished.
blockers.evidence: Mobile viewport measurements show input rect ~231.66px wide × 44px high; send rect ~59.34px wide × 13px high; voice rect ~44px wide × 16px high (iPhone SE portrait 375x667 state, metrics_summary.txt). The on-screen copy shows the Send badge and the mic button adjacent to a relatively large textarea, reinforcing the imbalance.
blockers.fix: Increase the height of the voice and Send controls to match a 44–48px baseline (e.g., set .ry-agent-btn to a fixed height of 44–48px and align vertically with the textarea). Consider resizing the voice button to a 44x44 square and adjusting horizontal padding so the textarea retains room.
blockers.location: mobile portrait and landscape, especially 375x667 and 393x852 states, in the input row (.ry-agent-input-row)

blockers.description: Transcript area appears sparsely populated on first load, with a single assistant message and a large empty panel below it, which reads as a non-live workspace.
blockers.evidence: iPhone SE portrait shows a greeting message but a large portion of the transcript container remains empty; metrics indicate the transcript block starts around top ~180px with substantial vertical space beneath the first message. Visible copy includes: 'Hi. I\'m the hq agent. Tell me what you want to do, or ask me what to focus on.'
blockers.fix: Preload or seed the transcript with a compact starter prompt or actions (e.g., Today’s tasks) so the main workspace feels immediately active. Alternatively move starter prompts into the transcript as removable cards to reduce perceived emptiness.
blockers.location: portrait states (e.g., iPhone SE 375x667, iPhone 14 Pro 393x852)

blockers.description: Avatar remains visually prominent in mobile portrait, competing with the workspace hierarchy and reducing perceived focus on the chat area.
blockers.evidence: Code defines the avatar at 96x96px (CSS in agent-shell); screenshots show a large circular avatar above the transcript in mobile states, drawing initial attention away from the chat area.
blockers.fix: Downgrade the avatar prominence on mobile (e.g., reduce to ~72px or move avatar into a compact identity row above the transcript) so the transcript and input row become the first visual priority.

blockers.description: Starter chip row appears cropped/partially obscured on narrow viewports, reducing perceived affordance of quick prompts.
blockers.evidence: Chips row shows multiple pills including 'What\'s on my schedule tomorrow?', 'Anything I haven\'t responded to?', etc. In narrow portrait, the second chip text is partially clipped (e.g., 'Anything I haven\'t...' or 'Anything I ha...'). Screenshot 'iphone_se_portrait.png' exhibits this behavior.
blockers.fix: Add a right-edge fade or limit visible chips with a clear horizontal cue; or move some chips into the transcript area as suggested prompts to reduce cropping and improve readability.
blockers.location: chip row (.ry-agent-scenarios) in mobile portrait states (375x667, 393x852)

blockers.description: Top header chrome (Don't auto-show today; mute; close) reads as floating chrome rather than integrated header, impacting perceived polish.
blockers.evidence: Screenshots show a separate rounded header pill at top-left with 'DON'T AUTO-SHOW TODAY' and circular action buttons on the right (mute and close); this chrome reads as a decorative overlay rather than a cohesive header region.
blockers.fix: Consolidate header into a single, compact top utility bar with consistent padding; move the text into a small label and place actions (mute/close) in a single row with equal visual weight to feel like part of the overlay.
blockers.location: top header area across mobile states

polish.description: Composer controls are visually underweight relative to the main text area
polish.fix: Increase height of Send and Voice buttons to align with the textarea baseline (44–48px). Consider standardizing button height across all mobile states and adjusting horizontal padding so the CTA area feels balanced.
Seed the transcript with a compact starter prompt or incorporate starter prompts into the transcript so the workspace appears active from first load.
Reduce avatar size to ~72px on mobile or relocate into a compact identity row so the workspace remains the primary focus.

polish.description: Chip row readability on narrow viewports
polish.fix: Add a subtle right-edge fade or set a small, visible hint that chips are horizontally scrollable; consider reducing the number of chips shown or reflowing some prompts into the transcript area.
Create a compact header bar for the top controls (Don't auto-show today, mute, close) with consistent padding and alignment to feel part of the overlay rather than floating UI.

strengths: Overlay fits mobile viewports without hard clipping across tested states (no 0px shell overflow observed).
100dvh and safe-area insets are applied, improving keyboard and notch handling.
Avatar sizing has been reduced from 140px to 96px on mobile, which improves readability and space efficiency.
Transcript and chips remain navigable via horizontal scrolling, supporting quick prompts on small screens.
No runtime console errors observed during viewport tests.
